examples: Convergence beat: expanded Jaina's reaction after Sylvanas confirms the supplier pattern so the chapter's hinge lands with more weight.
Granary handoff: softened 'Millhaven's old man had a brother here after all' to 'had company here after all' so the voice stays suspicious instead of prematurely conclusive.
Captain-scale beat: changed 'The word changed the captain differently than Millhaven had' to a plainer Jaina-shaped line about Millhaven being horror and Andorhal being scale.
Closing beat: kept Sylvanas dry in 'Then we'll follow whoever keeps sharpening the blade' without letting the line slide into theatrical menace.
observations: Lines 15-37: Jaina's early dialogue stays exact, irritable, and fast rather than grand or modern.
Lines 47-79: Sylvanas handles the captain in clipped finished judgments that match her voice sheet.
Lines 91-109: Jaina's medic exchange uses short, built questions and downshifted explanation appropriate for civilians.
Lines 227-269: the chapter's central method-convergence now carries enough pause to feel like a real correction in Jaina's thinking.
Lines 517-549: the closing exchange keeps the relationship cold and functional, not softened into trust.

examples: Tomas death beat: replaced 'He had thought he would have longer between the choice and the dark' with visible inference only, keeping the chapter inside Jaina's knowledge.
observations: Lines 3-13: opening stays inside Jaina's sensory read of ordinary weather hiding buried wrongness.
Lines 41-65: the captain is read through face, posture, and speech rather than detached narrator summary.
Lines 173-181: Jaina infers 'granary keeper, probably. Or baker.' instead of being granted certainty she could not have.
Lines 307-317: necromantic craft recognition remains Jaina's trained read, not author commentary.
Lines 435-449: the poison death now resolves through what Jaina sees and attempts, with no leak into Tomas's interiority.

examples: Supplier callback: trimmed the Millhaven echo so the newer grain-seller thread remains weak evidence rather than solved identity proof.
notes: Chapter 3's reduced quartet remains intact: Jaina, Sylvanas, Cyndia, and Lyriel only.
Neral and the survivor girl stay off-stage as required memory residue, not accidentally reintroduced in-scene.
Jaina's broad necromantic knowledge remains established; the new layer is skilled applied design in the liquid concentrate.
Millhaven remains aftermath while this village is the first living-site evolution of the same pattern.
The Jaina-Sylvanas lane still advances as method dependence and irritation, not warmth.

examples: Convergence sequence: added two short paragraphs after 'It fits exactly' to break the procedural rush and let the hinge breathe.
Interrogation chain: inserted the fast mouth-and-body check beat after the arrow comes out so the scene stops snapping from accusation to custody with no middle pressure.
Andorhal reveal: recast the captain's reaction in a cleaner cadence so the scale shift lands in two blunt sentences instead of one abstract one.
observations: Lines 3-13: the opening alternates long travel sentences with hard short beats effectively.
Lines 91-109: the medic entrance uses clipped beat-stacks without collapsing into monotone.
Lines 131-147: the warded-child beat balances short declaratives with one longer explanatory turn.
Lines 279-317: the granary run keeps impact-chain compression while retaining one longer orienting sentence for the spill analysis.
Lines 541-558: the close lengthens just enough for aftertaste, then ends on a blunt Andorhal image.

examples: Cross-chapter opening: verified the two-day bridge from chapter3 and preserved the reduced quartet plus Millhaven residue in the first page.
Granary prisoner chain: added an immediate mouth/sleeve/belt/boot check before interrogation so Tomas's later poison death reads as trained discipline, not escort carelessness.
continuity.crossChapter: Chapter 4 opens with the same southbound pursuit after chapter3, carrying Millhaven's proof forward without recap and keeping the emotional residue as pressure rather than summary.
observations: Lines 3-13: opening acknowledges the planned two-day travel bridge from chapter3.
Lines 53-77: Millhaven's proof is used as leverage with the captain instead of being re-explained for the reader.
Lines 223-277: Jaina's grain answer and Sylvanas's supplier read now converge before the granary push, matching the plan's required method alignment.
Lines 357-467: Andorhal emerges from sacks and ledgers on-page, preserving the chapter5 handoff logic.
Lines 471-558: departure keeps village containment orders visible so the pursuit does not erase local stakes.

examples: Replaced 'one corner of the machine' with 'one corner of the work' to remove an unnecessary abstract/mechanical metaphor.
Removed one over-neat explanatory line in the convergence section by letting the unpleasant dependence land through paragraph shape instead of gloss.
observations: Lines 87-99: infirmary opening starts on pressure and smell, not throat-clearing scene setup.
Lines 189-215: bread-discovery beat avoids re-explaining the same clue twice once the loaf proves hot.
Lines 307-317: concentrate analysis keeps Jaina inside bodily and magical consequence rather than report voice.
Lines 403-417: the captain-nephew fallout stays brisk and specific rather than melodramatic.
Lines 493-513: village containment orders retain concrete action instead of summary abstraction.
Jaina explains the mechanism to civilians in plain field language: bread, flour, belly, recent stock.
Sylvanas's orders stay military-plain: seal the granary, post men, choose whether to command or bury.
The medic's speech remains practical and local, never drifting into modern clinical jargon.
Tomas sounds frightened and low-ranking, not like a cult functionary delivering exposition cleanly.
The final road exchange keeps both women in lived speech rather than memo-speak.
